steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The phrase "thick curtain of long, coily hair that...engulfs her" signals that the hair is so voluminous it nearly covers the girl completely.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Substitute each word back into "a thick curtain of long, coily hair that ______ engulfs her":

A. Cheerfully is an adverb of emotion or manner. Hair cannot engulf someone cheerfully; the sentence describes extent, not mood.

B. Doubtfully means with uncertainty or skepticism. The hair's coverage is a physical fact observed by the narrator — there is no doubt involved.

C. Almost means very nearly but not completely. "Hair that almost engulfs her" captures the visual image of being nearly swallowed by voluminous hair. ✓

D. Never means at no time. "Hair that never engulfs her" contradicts the sentence — the whole point is that the hair nearly does engulf her.

C is correct.
